title: SACRAMENTO (October 9, 2023) – With the support of Governor Newsom and persistence from the state legislature, California has passed AB 363 authored by Assemblymember Rebecca Bauer
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: California has passed AB 363, which bans over the counter sales of lawn and garden neonicotinoid pesticides by 2025.
Neonicotinoids are likely the most ecologically destructive pesticides since DDT.
Neonicotinoids are phenomenally toxic to bees and other insects.
Neonicotinoids can persist in soil for years and are easily carried long distances by rain or irrigation water.
Neonicotinoids extensively contaminate California surface waters.
Neonicotinoids are neurotoxins that can affect sensitive regions of the human brain and central nervous system.
Neonicotinoids can pass from mother to fetus.
Health experts warn that there may be no safe level of neonicotinoid exposure.
A recent study found neonicotinoid exposure in over 95% of pregnant women tested in California and four other states.
AB 363 will reduce public exposure to neonicotinoids by eliminating commonly used over the counter lawn and garden neonic products.
The bill requires the California Department of Pesticide Regulation to evaluate the impacts of remaining lawn and garden uses of neonicotinoids.
California's urban and suburban waters are contaminated by non-agricultural uses of neonicotinoids.
The review required by AB 363 will provide California with information necessary to address threats posed to 1,600 native bee species.
The health of California's diverse ecosystems is threatened by the use of neonicotinoids.
